pi_items.source: task.json metadata line 16 and setup_task.sh line 18 - delegate user created with explicit username in USER_PAYLOAD JSON
task.json metadata line 17 and setup_task.sh lines 19-20 - delegate user created with firstName='Sally' and lastName='Acm' in USER_PAYLOAD JSON
task.json metadata line 18 claims 5, but verifier.py line 115 implements 'if 4 <= duration_days <= 10' which accepts minimum 4 days
task.json metadata line 19 claims 9, but verifier.py line 115 implements 'if 4 <= duration_days <= 10' which accepts maximum 10 days
task_utils.sh line 10 and setup_task.sh line 18 - LDAP domain used for usernames in test ArkCase environment
setup_task.sh line 53 and task_utils.sh lines 10-11 - admin account used for task automation
task.json line 4 - test environment using ArkCase on Kubernetes

privileged_info_summary: This task uses fully synthetic test data in a development ArkCase environment. The delegate user Sally Acm (sally-acm@dev.arkcase.com) is created as part of task setup via REST API. The metadata values for username and name are verified against setup_task.sh. However, the duration constraint metadata is contradicted by the verifier implementation: task.json claims min_duration_days=5 and max_duration_days=9, but verifier.py line 115 actually implements a range of 4-10 days inclusive (4 <= duration_days <= 10). The task description specifies a 7-day duration, which falls within both ranges. All other metadata (LDAP domain, admin credentials, environment ID) is verified against the configuration and setup scripts.
